level_signals: 5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or related roles
Expected to act as strategic thought partner to GTM leadership
Builds and deploys production systems independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, Business Systems, Workflow Engineering, Solutions Engineering, Product Operations, or related roles
Experience building internal tools, workflow automations, AI-powered systems, or operational infrastructure
Familiarity with APIs, automation frameworks, lightweight scripting, or AI workflow tooling
Experience working with GTM systems such as Salesforce, analytics tooling, forecasting systems, or workflow platforms
Strong analytical and operational problem-solving skills
Experience deploying systems used by operators in production environments
Ability to balance rapid experimentation with scalable operational design

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a strong systems and product mindset and enjoy turning ambiguous business problems into scalable solutions
Have experimented with AI-native development tooling such as Codex, agents, workflow orchestration tools, or automation platforms
Have experience with CRM systems, RevOps workflows, forecasting processes, analytics tooling, or GTM operations
Have experience building automations, internal tools, integrations, or AI workflows in production environments
Are highly analytical and comfortable using data to drive operational decisions
Thrive in ambiguity and can prioritize high-impact opportunities across multiple stakeholders

scope.surface: GTM Strategy & Operations — AI-native internal tooling and workflows for Sales, Solutions, and Revenue Operations
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / API (AI research and deployment company)
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of AI-native workflows across GTM organization
core_responsibilities: Identify opportunities where AI can improve the quality, speed, scale, and effectiveness of GTM operations and field execution
Design and deploy AI-native workflows that combine agents, automation, systems integrations, and human review
Build internal tooling and operational accelerators using OpenAI APIs, Codex, Salesforce, automation platforms, and lightweight applications
Rapidly prototype and iterate on solutions that improve forecasting, pipeline management, account planning, territory operations, deal execution, customer insights, and productivity
Partner directly with Sales, Solutions, RevOps, Systems, Finance, and Enablement teams to operationalize AI into day-to-day workflows
Build reusable 'golden workflows,' templates, and operational tooling that can scale across the GTM organization
Help define best practices, governance, and guardrails for deploying AI within GTM operational workflows
Drive adoption through hands-on training, pair building sessions, documentation, and direct collaboration with operators
Serve as a strategic thought partner to GTM leadership on how AI can reshape operational processes and field productivity

green_flags: Role sits at OpenAI, a frontier AI company with direct access to cutting-edge models and APIs
Clear scope: builder+operator hybrid role with real production impact on GTM workflows
Encourages candidates who thrive in ambiguity — signals openness to varied backgrounds
red_flags: Location and remote eligibility not stated anywhere in the JD
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not addressed
Buzzword-heavy framing may obscure day-to-day role clarity
Dual 'builder and operator' expectation is broad and may lead to role scope creep

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and strategic thought partner language)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; assumed false based on IC framing)
scope.business_model (inferred from OpenAI's known B2B/API product offerings)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The 'You might thrive in this role if you:' section uses soft language but lists items that closely overlap with the 'Experience' section's harder requirements (e.g., CRM experience, automation experience). Items in 'You might thrive' section treated as soft/nice-to-haves; items under 'Experience' treated as must-haves.
Location (city, remote eligibility, hybrid vs onsite) is entirely unstated in the JD body.
